e1_sentence: It is narrated by Abu Nadr that he learnt from a letter sent by a man from the  Aslam tribe, who was a Companion of the Holy Prophet (may peace be upon him)  and whose name was Abdullah ibn Abu Aufa, to Umar ibn 'Ubaidullah when the  latter marched upon Haruriyya (Khawarij) informing him that the Messenger of  Allah (may peace be upon him) in one of those days when he was confronting the  enemy waited until the sun had declined

e2_sentence: Then he stood up (to address the  people) and said: "O ye men, do not wish for an encounter with the enemy

e1_sentence: Then he stood up (to address the  people) and said: "O ye men, do not wish for an encounter with the enemy
e2_sentence: Then the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) stood up  (again) and said: "O Allah, Revealer of the Book, Disperser of the clouds,  Defeater of the hordes, put our enemy to rout and help us against them.
